Sunday Report

Week of Apr 6 – Apr 10, 2026
Generated Sun Apr 5, 2026 at 8:32 PM PDT
Market Regime
MIXED MEDIUM
Driver: RATES-DRIVEN
  • US10Y at 4.31% is the dominant constraint — duration-sensitive risk remains under pressure.
  • DXY at 100.03 is holding elevated, capping metals and limiting international equity upside.
  • VIX at 23.9 — not in stress territory, but cross-asset alignment is poor.
Market Quality
MIXED
TRADE SELECTIVELY
Volatility Stability VIX 23.9 — elevated but not crisis-level
Cross-Asset Alignment Rates and equities diverging — no clean read
Trend Agreement Energy leading; metals and indices range-bound
Chop Detection Index tape showing range compression

Cross-Asset Interpretation
  • Dollar (100.03) strength is the primary headwind for metals and international equities.
  • US10Y at 4.31% — rate expectations are keeping equity multiples compressed.
  • WTI ($112.46) holding above support; energy sector is the only clean trend on the board.
  • Gold ($4642.10) and silver ($72.15) are range-bound — dollar is the primary constraint.
  • USDJPY at ¥159.74 — carry unwind risk is elevated if US yields pull back materially.
  • VIX (23.9) elevated — risk-on entry quality is poor; size down.
What Matters This Week
Federal Reserve Speakers
Rate path guidance directly moves DXY, yields, and risk appetite for the week.
Avoid new positions before major Fed speeches — tone shock risk is high.
WTI Supply Picture
Oil at $112.46 needs supply-side confirmation to sustain the energy sector bid.
Watch XLE and OXY for inventory-driven continuation or reversal signal.
Equity Index Trend Resolution
SPY and QQQ need to confirm direction — range compression is unsustainable.
Wait for directional break before adding index beta. No anticipation.
Event Calendar — High Impact Only
TBD Fed Speakers HIGH Tone on rate path — significant DXY and yield mover.
TBD Oil Inventories (EIA) MEDIUM Surprise vs consensus drives WTI and XLE direction.
Weekly Trade Themes
1 Energy continuation on supply tightness Bullish
WTI at $112.46 remains supported by OPEC discipline. XLE and OXY showing relative strength vs broader tape.
WTI loses prior support level or DXY accelerates above 106.
2 Rates-driven equity multiple compression Bearish
US10Y at 4.31% continues to cap growth multiples. QQQ underperforming on duration sensitivity.
US10Y breaks below 4.0% on a macro catalyst or Fed pivot signal.
3 Metals consolidation under strong dollar Neutral
Gold at $4642.10 and silver at $72.15 are range-bound. Dollar strength is suppressing the upside.
DXY breaks below 103 or Fed signals a dovish shift.
Energy Sector
XLE $59.25 -3.7%
OXY $62.97 -2.2%
XOM $160.69 -2.9%
WTI $112.46 +12.9%
XLE is maintaining relative strength with WTI at $112.46 providing underlying support. OXY at $62.97 is tracking futures with normal beta — no dislocation. Leverage ETFs (GUSH, UCO) viable for momentum continuation but require tight stops and defined risk.
Prioritize pullback entries in XLE and OXY — no chasing breakouts.
Metals Sector
XAU $4642.10 +3.3%
XAG $72.15 +3.8%
GDX $94.59 +14.8%
SLV $65.79 +8.3%
Gold at $4642.10 is consolidating with real yields elevated and dollar strong. Silver at $72.15 showing limited participation — gold/silver ratio is widening. GDX at $94.59 — miners are underperforming the metal, suggesting distribution pressure or hedging.
Avoid chasing metals until DXY shows a sustained break below 103.
Execution Playbook for the Week
  • Prioritize pullback entries in energy — no chasing breakouts above resistance
  • Avoid metals unless DXY weakens materially or real yields roll over
  • Reduce index size until trend alignment improves across SPY, QQQ, IWM
  • Favor spreads over outright directional calls in a mixed regime
  • No new positions within 30 min of major macro events (Fed, CPI, NFP)
  • Size down to 50% max while VIX remains above 18
Summary
Regime MIXED Quality MIXED Posture TRADE SELECTIVELY
Primary Drivers
  • Rates direction — US10Y is the regime pin
  • Dollar strength — DXY is suppressing risk appetite
Top Themes
  • Energy continuation on supply tightness
  • Rates-driven equity multiple compression
  • Metals consolidation under strong dollar
Key Risk Hawkish Fed surprise re-accelerating yields above 4.5% and triggering broad risk-off.
